rationale: Keep: the cited 2006 JMR paper directly investigates enjoyment in shared hedonic experiences (congruence/incongruence social influence), which closely overlaps the citing paper’s core question about when shared experiences increase or decrease enjoyment. The citing note explicitly says it extends prior shared-experience research from more structured paradigms, and the cited study’s lab shared-viewing/tasting paradigm fits that likely foundational prior work rather than an incidental mention.
